10225.Summary: The protein encoded by this gene belongs to the immunoglobulin superfamily. It is a type I membrane protein. The protein may play a role in the adhesive interactions of activated T and NK cells during the late phase of the immune response. It may also function in antigen presentation. Alternative splicing generates multiple transcript variants encoding distinct isoforms. [provided by RefSeq, Jan 2016]
10225.mRNA_(NM)_and_protein_(NP)_sequences: NM_005816 -> NP_005807, T-cell surface protein tactile
